topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

My thumb hovered over the screen, a familiar, almost involuntary twitch. I meant to send a quick reminder to Sarah about the meeting time. Instead, my finger landed squarely on the wrong contact. A jolt of ice shot up my spine, making my breath catch in my throat. I stared at the screen, the words I’d typed—a casual scheduling note—now glaring back at me from an unfamiliar name. My jaw tightened, the muscles in my neck bunching. I quickly swiped back, a frantic attempt to erase the digital blunder, but the residue of the mistake clung to my skin like a film. I felt a sudden, sharp need to scrub my eyes, as if the light itself had become offensive.

[story 2]

The notification sound was jarringly loud in the quiet cafe. I glanced down, my heart immediately sinking into a heavy, unpleasant pit. It was a message I hadn't intended to send, a clumsy typo directed at someone entirely inappropriate. A wave of nausea washed over me, making my stomach clench painfully. I pulled my hand back from the phone as if it were suddenly contaminated. My shoulders slumped, a physical manifestation of the sudden, profound unease. I wanted to melt into the worn upholstery of the chair, wishing the entire interaction—the sending, the potential reception—could simply vanish.

[story 3]

I reread the text, the words mocking me from the display. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck, staining my cheeks a deep crimson. Every nerve ending seemed to be screaming in protest. The air in the room felt suddenly thick and suffocating. I pressed my palms flat against the table, gripping the edges until my knuckles turned white. There was a deep, visceral recoil, a strong urge to physically distance myself from the device, to sever the connection entirely. I couldn't look at the screen for another moment; the sight of it felt like an affront to everything clean and orderly within me.
